name: Pineapple Upside-Down Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Using a large cast-iron skillet, melt margarine on top of the stove.
2. Add brown sugar and spread evenly with a fork until it forms a "slushy" mixture about 1/4-inch deep.
3. Place pineapple slices and cherries on top of the brown sugar mixture.
4. In a bowl, mix the cake mix, 2 eggs, and reserved pineapple juice using an electric mixer until well combined. The batter will be thick, heavy, and fluffy.
5. Spread the batter around on top of the pineapple mixture in the skillet.
6. Bake at 350°F for approximately 25 minutes, or until done.
7. Using heavy hot pads or mitts, remove the cake from the oven.
8. Loosen the cake at the edges by running a knife around the edge of the skillet.
9. Carefully place a large plate on top of the skillet and quickly turn it over to dump the cake onto the plate. Let cool.
10. Keep covered after the cake has cooled.
